title: Ixeris alpicola
intro: Ixeris alpicola, also known as Alpine wormwood or Japanese wormwood, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Ixeris alpicola contains bioactive constituents like sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in modern herbal preparations to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Ixeris alpicola is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is native to mountainous regions and is often found in rocky outcrops and grasslands. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Ixeris alpicola is further classified within the tribe Cichorieae and genus Ixeris. The classification of Ixeris alpicola places it as a distinct species within this family. The exact classification of Ixeris alpicola may be subject to further research and reclassification as botanical knowledge evolves.
